title: Custom Roles & Permissions System — data-driven RBAC, Team Lead role, bespoke external account types
scope:
Replace Vector's hardcoded three-value user_role Postgres ENUM with a data-driven roles + permissions + role_permissions schema, refactor 4 layers of consumers (DB triggers, Go middleware, 21 frontend files, seeded migrations), introduce a fourth system role (Team Lead, rank 20), seed the Team Lead account (team_lead@mmffdev.com), and ship gadmin/padmin-facing UI to create custom roles, assign permission sets, and provision bespoke account types for external users (third-party agents, contractors, auditors).

This plan turns R030's evidence base into action. The full A–W workflow with ADD/UPDATE/REMOVE badges, action descriptions, and acceptance criteria is enumerated under workflow_table below.

Architecture in one paragraph. A new roles table keyed on UUID with optional subscription_id (NULL = system role; non-null = tenant-custom). A permissions catalogue seeded from a Go-side source of truth. A role_permissions junction. users.role ENUM column replaced with users.role_id uuid. page_roles.role ENUM column replaced with page_roles.role_id uuid. RequireRole middleware replaced with RequirePermission("code"). The static Rank() switch in Go is deleted; rank is read from the role row. The role-ceiling sentinel still fires on rank inequality — only the source moves from a typed-string switch to a row read. The 21 frontend role-equality checks become permission lookups via useHasPermission(code). Custom roles are tenant-scoped, RLS-friendly, and visible only inside their owning tenant.

Trust-No-One preserved. Tenant ID always from the verified session, never from the payload. Target role ID arrives in the payload but is validated server-side against the actor's users.create.<role_id> permission AND the role-ceiling rank check — both gates must pass. System roles are immutable from the API; only their label and description can be edited (for localisation). Self-elevation is forbidden — an actor cannot edit their own role at all. Permission catalogue is server-authoritative; tenants compose roles out of it but cannot invent permissions. Hardcoded protected human accounts (CLAUDE.md HARD RULE) untouched — their role_id simply points at the seeded system role row post-migration.

Creator matrix in MVP. G_admin (rank 30) creates anyone: G_admin, P_admin, Team Lead, User, custom roles. P_admin (rank 25) creates Team Leads and Users only. Team Lead (rank 20) and User (rank 10) cannot create accounts. Custom roles get users.create.<target_role_id> grants explicitly per role. Single-role-per-user in MVP; multi-role deferred to Phase X.

What this plan does NOT do (Phase X deferrals). No multi-role-per-user. No tri-state Allow/Deny/Inherit (binary only). No reusable named permission schemes (clone-and-edit only). No external-IdP gating, session-length caps, or time-bound provisioning for external roles (the is_external flag is metadata only in MVP). No NOTIFY/LISTEN realtime cache invalidation (TTL of 60s is enough). No per-workspace role overrides (existing user_workspace_permissions ACL covers that axis).
value:
• Unblocks Team Lead and every future role. The original ask — team_lead@mmffdev.com — takes one row to provision once the schema is data-driven; without this work it's a compile-time enum change that ripples across 80+ files.
  • Tenants get role autonomy. G_admins shape their org's permissions without filing a ticket against Vector engineering — same value Jira's permission schemes deliver on Premium and Enterprise tiers, but offered to every tenant.
  • Bespoke external-account types. Third-party agents, contractors, and auditors can be modelled as first-class roles with bounded permission sets — the gap that pushes regulated customers off Rally and Azure DevOps onto seat-by-seat carve-outs.
  • Compliance posture improves. Every role/permission grant is audited; least-privilege is the default; the permission catalogue is closed and server-authoritative; system roles are immutable; self-elevation is forbidden. SOC2 / ISO 27001 evidence becomes a one-screen export.
  • Removes a hidden tax on every feature ticket. Today, “hide this from non-admins” means editing the role union, the middleware allowlist, and 21 frontend files. After this work it's a permission entry plus a RequirePermission on the route.
  • Page registry becomes fully dynamic. The existing pages + page_roles tables stop carrying an enum and start carrying role IDs — tenants can grant menu visibility to custom roles without code changes.
  • Sets up Phase X cleanly. Multi-role-per-user, named permission schemes, time-bound provisioning, and external-IdP gating are all schema-additive on top of the v1 design — no rework of v1 to enable them.
  • Beats Rally on flexibility. Rally's five-level fixed ladder is the competitor's known weakness; Vector's data-driven design with custom roles addresses it directly.
  • Beats Jira on accessibility. Custom roles in Jira are gated to Premium/Enterprise plans; Vector ships them universally.
implementation_plan: MVP cut (locked 2026-05-03): four system roles (gadmin rank 30, padmin rank 25, team_lead rank 20, user rank 10) plus one external-archetype seed (external rank 5). Custom roles tenant-scoped with rank in [11..24] or [26..29] (system bands reserved). Single-role-per-user. Binary permissions (no Allow/Deny/Inherit). Permission cache TTL 60s. Hardcoded protected human accounts unchanged. Team Lead seeded with the same permission set as P_admin in v0 (per the user's 'same rights as padmin for now' direction); ranks differ (20 vs 25) so role-ceiling produces the right answer when permissions diverge.

Migration A: roles table — UUID PK, subscription_id nullable FK (NULL=system, non-null=tenant-custom), code text, label text, description text, rank int (CHECK rank>0; tenant rank in [11..24] or [26..29]), is_system bool, is_external bool, archived_at, audit columns. Unique (subscription_id, code). Tenant-scoped index.

Migration B: permissions table — UUID PK, code text UNIQUE (e.g. 'users.create.team_lead'), label, category, description. Seeded from Go catalogue at migration time; startup-time check fails if a code-declared permission is missing.
Migration C: role_permissions junction — (role_id, permission_id) PK, granted_by FK users, granted_at. Seeded for system roles per the catalogue table in R030.

Migration D: seed system roles + their permission bags — gadmin/padmin/team_lead/user/external rows with stable UUIDs (recorded in Go constants for tests); role_permissions seeded per the R030 §7.2 table.

Migration E: users.role_id — add nullable FK column, backfill from users.role enum (SELECT id FROM roles WHERE code = users.role::text AND subscription_id IS NULL), set NOT NULL after verified, KEEP users.role column for one full release cycle for rollback.
Migration F: page_roles.role_id — add nullable FK column, backfill from page_roles.role enum, set NOT NULL, drop old role column (page_roles is internal).
Migration G: provision_on_first_gadmin trigger — rewrite to compare against the seeded gadmin role row by code, not the enum literal; idempotent re-run safe.
Migration H: bookmarks.go fan-out — replace hand-written `[]models.Role{...}` slice with a SELECT id FROM roles WHERE subscription_id IS NULL OR subscription_id = $1.

Backend A: delete `type Role string` constants and `Rank()` switch; introduce `type Role struct { ID uuid.UUID; Code string; Label string; Rank int; SubscriptionID *uuid.UUID; IsSystem bool; IsExternal bool }` with a tenant-scoped cache loaded on session create and refreshed on role change.

Backend B: replace RequireRole middleware with RequirePermission(perm string); the actor's permission set is loaded once per session token, cached for 60s, and consulted on every request. Cache invalidation on role mutation broadcasts to in-process listeners.

Backend C: refactor 14+ RequireRole(models.Role*) call sites in cmd/server/main.go to RequirePermission('users.list'), RequirePermission('roles.create'), etc. — mechanical translation per the catalogue table.

Backend D: roles service — internal/roles/service.go with List, Get, Create, Update, Archive, AssignPermissions, RevokePermissions; server-enforces tenant scoping, role-ceiling on edits (actor's rank >= target role's rank), system-role immutability (only label/description editable), and self-elevation prohibition. Sentinel errors: ErrRoleNotFound, ErrSystemRoleImmutable, ErrSelfElevation, ErrPermissionDenied.

Backend E: permissions catalogue — internal/permissions/catalogue.go declares every permission as a typed Go constant; package init() seeds the permissions table on first run and validates parity at startup (refuses to start if catalogue and table diverge).

Backend F: role-ceiling generalisation — backend/internal/users/service.go, all 5 sites, swap actor.Role.Rank() for actor.role.Rank (struct field) loaded by the auth middleware. Same comparison; new data source.

Backend G: REST surface for roles — GET /api/roles (list, tenant-scoped + system), POST /api/roles (create custom, gadmin only, payload: code/label/rank/is_external), PATCH /api/roles/:id (rename/relabel/rerank), DELETE /api/roles/:id (soft-archive, tenant custom only), POST /api/roles/:id/permissions (grant), DELETE /api/roles/:id/permissions/:permId (revoke). All role-ceiling-checked, all audited.

Backend H: user-create payload validation — POST /api/users intercepts the target role_id and refuses with 403 unless actor has users.create.<target_role_id>. Existing role-ceiling check remains as second gate (belt-and-braces).

Frontend A: replace Role TS union in app/contexts/AuthContext.tsx — new type Role = { id; code; label; rank; is_system; is_external }; new useAuth() returns { user; role; permissions: Set<string> }; new useHasPermission(code) hook.

Frontend B: refactor 21 files doing role === 'gadmin' / role === 'padmin' equality — replace each with useHasPermission('<perm>') driven by the actual intent (menu.admin.view / roles.create / users.archive / etc). Add ESLint rule banning role === <string-literal> compares; CI fails on reintroduction.
Frontend C: drop roleLabels: Record<Role,string> constants in 2 files; render role.label from the row.

Frontend D: /admin/roles list page — gadmin only, tenant-scoped + system view; Panel-wrapped per PLA-0005 addressables; columns: label, code, rank, is_system, is_external, member count; actions: create, edit, archive (custom only).

Frontend E: /admin/roles/:id detail page — name/label/rank/external editor; permission grid (groups by category); audit trail panel; member list with link to user detail. System roles: editable label/description only, all other fields read-only.

Frontend F: user-create / user-edit role picker — replaces the current closed-set dropdown with a server-filtered list of roles the actor can create (returned from a new GET /api/roles/creatable). External-flagged roles render under a separate 'External' section.

Backend I: bespoke external-account type seed — external system role at rank 5 with minimal permission set (portfolio.list only); is_external=true; tenant-customisable via clone-and-edit (POST /api/roles with clone_from=<role_id>).

Frontend G: external-account UX affordance — chip in user table for is_external roles, separate filter on /admin/users, copy adjustments per R030 §9 (the long-term IdP/session-cap features stay in Phase X but UI hooks are in place).

Backend J: audit log integration — new action codes (role_created, role_updated, role_archived, permissions_granted, permissions_revoked, user_role_changed); same audit table; queryable via existing reports.
Backend K: cache invalidation — on roles.update / role_permissions write, broadcast to in-process listeners that hold permission set caches; sessions for users in that role refresh on next request.
Tests A: tenant isolation — tenant A's custom role invisible to tenant B (server returns 404 on direct GET, omitted from list).
Tests B: role-ceiling — P_admin attempting to create G_admin via direct API returns 403 even when payload claims otherwise; permission gate fires first, rank gate fires second.
Tests C: creator-matrix — Team Lead and User cannot reach POST /api/users at all (403 via RequirePermission); P_admin can create Team Lead/User but not P_admin/G_admin.
Tests D: system-role immutability — PATCH /api/roles/<gadmin_id> with rank=5 returns 422; same with code change; label change succeeds.
Tests E: self-elevation prohibition — actor cannot edit own role; cannot grant own role new permissions.
Tests F: cache invalidation — user X has perm Y; admin revokes perm Y; within 60s X's next request returns 403.
Tests G: permission-catalogue parity — startup test fails if a Go-declared permission is not in the seeded permissions table or vice versa.

Tests H: hardcoded human accounts — gadmin@/padmin@/user@ rows have role_id pointing at the correct seeded system role post-migration; their password_hash, email, role_id, is_active are unchanged from pre-migration values.

Tests I: full e2e creation chain — gadmin creates Team Lead account team_lead@mmffdev.com / password123!; gadmin creates a custom 'External Auditor' role with portfolio.list only and is_external=true; gadmin creates a user under that role; the user logs in, sees only what the role permits, fails on every other route.

Backend L: protected accounts seed — verify migration leaves gadmin@/padmin@/user@ rows untouched in every credential field (password_hash, email, is_active, role_id maps to correct system role, password_changed_at preserved). Migration includes a post-step assertion query that fails the migration if any protected account row's credentials differ from pre-migration.
Provision: insert team_lead@mmffdev.com with password123!; document in <accounts> reference; do NOT touch the existing protected human accounts.

Migration Z: drop users.role enum column (deferred — runs in a follow-up release after one full cycle of monitoring confirms zero reads of the old column). The user_role enum type itself stays in pg_type until the column is dropped, then DROP TYPE.

Docs: docs/c_c_roles_permissions.md leaf — schema, permission catalogue, creator matrix, immutability rules, audit shape, cache invalidation, Phase X deferrals; CLAUDE.md pointer; cross-link to R030 and PLA-0007 in c_scope.md.
areas_impacted: SQL: 9 migrations (A–H plus Z deferred drop) — roles, permissions, role_permissions, role_id columns on users + page_roles, trigger rewrite, bookmarks fan-out replacement, eventual enum drop.

API: new internal/roles Go service (sole writer for roles + role_permissions); new internal/permissions catalogue with startup parity check; ~7 new REST endpoints; refactored RequirePermission middleware replaces RequireRole on 14+ existing routes.

UI: new /admin/roles list and /admin/roles/:id detail pages (Panel + addressables); refactored 21 frontend files to use useHasPermission; user-create role picker rewrite; external-account chip and filter.

DEV: ESLint rule banning role === string-literal; CI parity test for permission catalogue; e2e suite covering creator matrix, role ceiling, system-role immutability, cache invalidation, protected-account preservation.
GOV: every role/permission mutation lands in the audit log; gadmin can review who created/edited/granted what and when.

SEC: trust-no-one boundary at internal/roles.Service — direct INSERTs/UPDATEs to roles or role_permissions outside the service forbidden; tenant scoping baked into every query; system roles immutable via API; self-elevation rejected; permission catalogue server-authoritative.
AUD: new audit action codes (role_created, role_updated, role_archived, permissions_granted, permissions_revoked, user_role_changed); existing audit table reused.
DOC: one new leaf doc (c_c_roles_permissions.md) plus index pointer plus scope row plus R030 cross-reference.
feature_list: roles table — system + tenant-custom, ranked, tenant-scoped, soft-archive, is_external flag.
permissions catalogue — closed Go-side source of truth, seeded into table, startup parity check.
role_permissions junction — auditable grants, granted_by tracked.
users.role_id replaces users.role enum — backfilled, NOT NULL, old column kept for one cycle.
page_roles.role_id replaces page_roles.role enum — same pattern.
Team Lead seeded as fourth system role at rank 20.
External seeded as system archetype at rank 5 for tenant clone-and-edit.
internal/roles.Service Go module — sole writer; tenant-scoped; role-ceiling enforced; self-elevation forbidden.
RequirePermission middleware — replaces RequireRole on all 14+ routes; permission set cached per session 60s.
Permission catalogue parity startup check — refuses to start if Go and DB diverge.
REST surface — /api/roles CRUD plus permissions grant/revoke plus /api/roles/creatable for user-create filtering.
/admin/roles list and detail UI — Panel-wrapped, addressable, gadmin-only, system-role label/description-only edit gate.
useHasPermission frontend hook + ESLint rule banning role === string-literal compares.
User-create role picker — server-filtered to roles the actor can create; external roles in a separate section.
Cache invalidation broadcast on role / role_permissions mutations.
Audit log integration for all role + permission events.
Protected-human-account preservation guard — migration assertion verifies gadmin@/padmin@/user@ credential fields unchanged.
team_lead@mmffdev.com seeded with password123!, role=team_lead.
Bespoke external-account UX — is_external flag drives chip, filter, and clone-and-edit flow.
docs/c_c_roles_permissions.md leaf doc.
features_extended: R030 turns into action: the blocker map (DB / Go / 21 frontend files / hardcoded constraints) and the prior-art synthesis (Jira / Rally / Azure DevOps / RBAC best practices) drive the schema, the permission catalogue, and the immutability + tenant-isolation rules in this plan.
PLA-0005 extends: /admin/roles and /admin/roles/:id adopt the addressables substrate from day one; every Panel is <Panel>-wrapped; each surface registers an addressable id.
page_roles dynamic gating extends: the existing pages + page_roles infrastructure becomes fully dynamic — tenants can grant menu visibility to custom roles without code changes.
Audit log extends: existing audit table gains five new action codes for role + permission events; no schema change required.
features_removed: user_role Postgres ENUM as the source of truth — superseded by the roles table; the enum type is dropped in Migration Z after a one-cycle observation window.
type Role string + Rank() switch in backend/internal/models/models.go — superseded by struct loaded from the role row.
RequireRole(roles ...models.Role) middleware — superseded by RequirePermission(perm string).
TS union type Role = 'user' | 'padmin' | 'gadmin' in app/contexts/AuthContext.tsx — superseded by struct + permission set.
Hand-written []models.Role{...} slice in nav/bookmarks.go:238 — superseded by SELECT from roles.
roleLabels: Record<Role,string> closed-set lookup in 2 files — superseded by reading role.label.
21 role === 'gadmin' equality checks across the frontend — superseded by useHasPermission (and an ESLint rule blocking reintroduction).

legend.ADD: Green badge — new entity (table, file, route, UI surface, or behaviour) introduced by this plan.
legend.UPDATE: Orange badge — existing entity is edited or refactored; original purpose preserved or generalised.
legend.REMOVE: Red badge — existing entity is removed; behaviour either replaced upstream or superseded by an ADD.

rows.description: Create roles table — UUID PK, nullable subscription_id (NULL=system), code, label, rank int with CHECK constraint, is_system bool, is_external bool, archived_at, audit columns. Unique (subscription_id, code). Tenant-scoped index.
rows.acceptance: Migration runs cleanly on dev/staging; SELECT returns zero rows pre-seed; goose status reports up-to-date; CHECK constraint rejects rank in reserved system bands for tenant-custom rows.

rows.description: Create permissions catalogue table — UUID PK, code text UNIQUE, label, category, description. Seeded from Go-side catalogue at migration time.
rows.acceptance: Migration creates the table; seed populates the row count expected by the Go catalogue; startup parity check passes; no permission codes outside the catalogue exist in the DB.

rows.description: Create role_permissions junction table — composite PK (role_id, permission_id), granted_by FK users, granted_at. Seeded with system-role permission bags per R030 §7.2.
rows.acceptance: Migration creates the table; gadmin row has roles.create / roles.assign_permissions / users.create.* permissions; padmin has users.create.team_lead and users.create.user but not users.create.gadmin or users.create.padmin; team_lead and user have no users.create.* permissions.

rows.description: Seed Team Lead system role (rank 20) and External archetype role (rank 5). Team Lead's permission set in v0 mirrors P_admin per the user's 'same rights as padmin for now' direction; ranks differ (20 vs 25) so role-ceiling produces correct semantics when permissions later diverge.
rows.acceptance: SELECT FROM roles WHERE code='team_lead' returns one row with rank=20, is_system=true, subscription_id NULL; SELECT FROM role_permissions WHERE role_id=team_lead_id returns the same permission set as the padmin role minus users.create.padmin.

rows.description: Add users.role_id uuid FK column. Backfill from users.role enum via SELECT id FROM roles WHERE code = users.role::text AND subscription_id IS NULL. KEEP users.role column for one release cycle for rollback.
rows.acceptance: All user rows have role_id set post-backfill; zero NULL role_id; users.role still readable; protected human accounts (gadmin@, padmin@, user@) point at the correct system role rows.

rows.description: Add page_roles.role_id uuid FK column. Backfill from page_roles.role enum. Drop old role column (page_roles is internal — no external readers, safe to drop in same migration).
rows.acceptance: All page_role rows have role_id; page registry continues to gate menu visibility correctly per role; smoke test: gadmin sees admin menu, user does not.

rows.description: Rewrite provision_on_first_gadmin trigger — compare against the seeded gadmin role row by code, not the enum literal. Idempotent re-run safe.
rows.acceptance: Trigger fires only when first user with role.code='gadmin' inserted; re-running migration is a no-op; existing tenants unaffected.

rows.description: Refactor backend/internal/nav/bookmarks.go:238 — replace `for _, r := range []models.Role{...}` with a SELECT from roles WHERE subscription_id IS NULL OR subscription_id = $1.
rows.acceptance: Bookmarks fan-out emits the same per-role bookmarks as before plus any tenant-custom roles; no hardcoded role identifiers remain in the file.

rows.description: Delete `type Role string` constants and `Rank()` switch from backend/internal/models/models.go.
rows.acceptance: go vet passes; no remaining references to RoleUser / RolePAdmin / RoleGAdmin constants in non-test code; no Rank() method on a typed string.

rows.description: Introduce new Role struct: { ID uuid.UUID; Code string; Label string; Rank int; SubscriptionID *uuid.UUID; IsSystem bool; IsExternal bool }. Loaded by auth middleware on session create; cached for 60s.
rows.acceptance: auth.UserFromCtx returns a *User with embedded *Role; downstream code reads actor.role.Rank as a struct field; cache hit rate >99% on steady-state load.

rows.description: Remove RequireRole(roles ...models.Role) middleware in backend/internal/auth/middleware.go.
rows.acceptance: Function deleted; no remaining call sites; build succeeds after step L lands.

rows.description: Add RequirePermission(perm string) middleware. Reads permission set from cached actor; rejects with 403 if missing.
rows.acceptance: Unit test: actor without perm gets 403; actor with perm passes; cache hit avoids DB on >99% of requests; cache invalidation on role mutation observed in test.

rows.description: Refactor 14+ RequireRole call sites in backend/cmd/server/main.go to RequirePermission with the catalogue code matching the route's intent (users.list / roles.create / etc).
rows.acceptance: Every prior RequireRole site replaced; integration test suite still passes (gadmin/padmin/user behaviours preserved); grep for RequireRole returns zero matches in non-test code.

rows.description: Refactor backend/internal/users/service.go — 5 sites reading actor.Role.Rank() now read actor.role.Rank as a struct field. Role-ceiling sentinel ErrRoleCeiling unchanged in name and meaning.
rows.acceptance: Existing role-ceiling tests pass unchanged; new test confirms the same enforcement on a tenant-custom role with rank between system rows (e.g. tenant-custom rank 22 cannot edit a padmin at rank 25).

rows.description: Add internal/roles Go service — List, Get, Create, Update, Archive, AssignPermissions, RevokePermissions. Sole writer for roles + role_permissions. Tenant-scoped queries, role-ceiling enforced on edits, system-role immutability gate, self-elevation prohibition. Sentinel errors: ErrRoleNotFound, ErrSystemRoleImmutable, ErrSelfElevation, ErrPermissionDenied.
rows.acceptance: Direct INSERT/UPDATE to roles or role_permissions outside this service flagged by lint:writer-boundary; system role label/description editable; system role rank/code/is_system not editable via API; self-edit attempt returns ErrSelfElevation.

rows.description: Add internal/permissions/catalogue.go — typed Go constants for every permission code; package init() seeds the permissions table on first run; startup parity check refuses to start if catalogue and table diverge.
rows.acceptance: Server start fails with a parity error if a Go constant is missing in DB or vice versa; CI test asserts catalogue has every entry from the R030 §7.2 table; adding a permission requires both a Go constant and a migration row (documented).

rows.description: Add REST surface for roles: GET /api/roles (list, tenant + system), POST /api/roles (create custom, gadmin only), PATCH /api/roles/:id, DELETE /api/roles/:id (soft-archive), POST /api/roles/:id/permissions, DELETE /api/roles/:id/permissions/:permId, GET /api/roles/creatable (filtered by actor's users.create.* perms — used by user-create form).
rows.acceptance: All endpoints return 403 without the matching permission; tenant isolation verified via penetration test (tenant A cannot see tenant B's roles); audit log records every mutation; system-role mutation attempts return 422 ErrSystemRoleImmutable.

rows.description: Update POST /api/users handler — payload's target role_id validated server-side against actor's users.create.<target_role_id> permission AND role-ceiling rank check. Both must pass. Failure returns 403 with which gate failed.
rows.acceptance: Penetration test: P_admin posts target role_id of gadmin, gets 403 (permission gate); P_admin posts custom role with rank 28 (above own 25), gets 403 (rank gate); both attempts audited.

rows.description: Refactor app/contexts/AuthContext.tsx — replace `type Role = 'user'|'padmin'|'gadmin'` union with struct type; useAuth returns { user, role, permissions: Set<string> }; new useHasPermission(code) hook backed by the permission set.
rows.acceptance: TypeScript compiles; useAuth consumers get a Role object with rank etc; useHasPermission returns boolean; closed-set Role union no longer importable from AuthContext.

rows.description: Refactor 21 frontend files doing `role === 'gadmin'` / `role === 'padmin'` equality — replace with useHasPermission('<perm>') matching the actual intent (menu.admin.view / roles.create / users.archive / etc). Drop the 2 `roleLabels` constants.
rows.acceptance: All 21 files migrated; ESLint rule banning role === string-literal compares passes in CI; visual smoke test of gadmin/padmin/user/team_lead login confirms menus and admin UI gate correctly.

rows.description: Build /admin/roles list page and /admin/roles/:id detail page. Panel-wrapped per PLA-0005; addressables registered. List shows label, code, rank, is_system, is_external, member count plus create/archive actions. Detail shows name editor, permission grid by category, audit trail panel, member list. System roles: only label and description editable.
rows.acceptance: Gadmin can create a tenant-custom role, assign permissions, see audit trail; cannot edit system role rank/code; padmin sees /admin/roles read-only (roles.list permission); user/team_lead get 403; address devtool finds a valid address for each panel.

rows.description: Refactor user-create / user-edit role picker — replace closed-set dropdown with a server-filtered list from /api/roles/creatable. External-flagged roles render in a separate 'External' section with a chip in the user table and a filter on /admin/users.
rows.acceptance: Gadmin sees gadmin/padmin/team_lead/user/custom roles in dropdown; padmin sees only team_lead/user (and any custom role they've been granted creator perms for); user-create endpoint refuses any role not in the actor's allowlist; external chip visible on /admin/users; filter narrows to is_external roles.

rows.description: Provision team_lead@mmffdev.com / password123! with role=team_lead via gadmin-authenticated POST /api/users. Document in <accounts> reference. Verify CLAUDE.md HARD RULE preserved: gadmin@mmffdev.com, padmin@mmffdev.com, user@mmffdev.com credential fields untouched throughout the plan.
rows.acceptance: team_lead@ row exists with role_id pointing at team_lead system role; password verifies via bcrypt; <accounts> doc updated; protected-account assertion test passes (gadmin@/padmin@/user@ password_hash, email, role_id, is_active, password_changed_at unchanged from pre-migration values); ESLint, build, and full e2e suite green.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Schema in place: roles, permissions, role_permissions; users.role_id and page_roles.role_id replace the enum-typed columns. provision_on_first_gadmin trigger and bookmarks fan-out reference roles via code/id, not enum literals. Migration Z (drop users.role) is documented but deferred; users.role retained for one release cycle.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Migrations A–H run cleanly; goose status up-to-date; SELECT count(*) FROM roles WHERE is_system shows the four core roles plus external; SELECT count(*) FROM users WHERE role_id IS NULL = 0; trigger smoke test fires on gadmin insert.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: internal/roles.Service is the sole writer for roles + role_permissions; system-role immutability and self-elevation gates are enforced server-side. Direct INSERT/UPDATE outside the service is blocked at lint/CI level.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: lint:writer-boundary passes; grep 'INSERT INTO roles' / 'UPDATE roles' outside backend/internal/roles returns zero matches; tests Tests-D (immutability) and Tests-E (self-elevation) pass.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: RequirePermission middleware replaces RequireRole on every route; permission catalogue parity check passes at startup; cache TTL is 60s with invalidation on role mutation.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: grep RequireRole in non-test code returns zero matches; permission catalogue startup test passes; cache-invalidation test (Tests-F) shows revoked permission denies within 60s.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Creator matrix is enforced: G_admin can create gadmin/padmin/team_lead/user/custom; P_admin can create team_lead/user; team_lead and user cannot create accounts. Server enforcement via users.create.<role_id> permission + role-ceiling; UI is convenience only.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Tests-C passes; penetration test (Tests-B) shows P_admin → G_admin POST returns 403 with permission-gate-fired audit row.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Tenant isolation: tenant A cannot see, edit, assign, or grant tenant B's custom roles. RLS-equivalent filtering is built into every roles query.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Tests-A passes; penetration test confirms direct-id GET returns 404 across tenants; list endpoint omits cross-tenant rows.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: 21 frontend files migrated to useHasPermission; ESLint rule blocks reintroduction of role === string-literal equality. /admin/roles UI exists and is accessible only to actors with roles.list / roles.create / roles.edit permissions per the role.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: ESLint rule passes in CI; visual smoke test of gadmin/padmin/team_lead/user logins confirms menus + admin UI gate correctly; address devtool finds valid addresses on /admin/roles panels.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Bespoke external account types work end-to-end: gadmin clones the External seed role into 'External Auditor', restricts permissions to portfolio.list, creates a user under that role, the user logs in and sees only what's permitted. is_external chip and filter render correctly.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Tests-I passes; manual e2e on staging confirms the auditor user can list portfolios but gets 403 on every other route.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Hardcoded protected human accounts (gadmin@, padmin@, user@) are byte-for-byte unchanged in their credential fields (password_hash, email, is_active, password_changed_at) post-migration; their role_id correctly points at the seeded system role row.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Tests-H passes; migration includes a post-step assertion that diffs protected-account credential fields against pre-migration values and aborts on any drift; CLAUDE.md HARD RULE compliance verified.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Audit log records every role + permission mutation with actor + IP + before/after; new action codes (role_created, role_updated, role_archived, permissions_granted, permissions_revoked, user_role_changed) are queryable via existing audit reports.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Audit smoke test: create custom role, edit it, archive it, and run an audit report — all five expected events present with correct actor and timestamps.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: team_lead@mmffdev.com is provisioned (password password123!) with role=team_lead and the <accounts> reference is updated. The Team Lead role's permission set in v0 matches P_admin's minus users.create.padmin (per user's 'same rights as padmin for now' direction).
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: User row exists; bcrypt verifies the password; <accounts> reference lists team_lead@ in the dev accounts table; logging in as team_lead@ permits the same actions as padmin@ except creating padmins.

risks.risk: Migration silently corrupts a protected human account's credentials. The HARD RULE is the most important invariant in the project — any drift on gadmin@/padmin@/user@ password_hash, email, is_active, role_id, or password_changed_at is a P0 incident.
risks.mitigation: Migration L (per the workflow) includes a post-step assertion that diffs every credential field of the three protected accounts against a pre-migration snapshot and aborts the migration with a hard error if anything differs. The role_id update is the only permitted change. CLAUDE.md HARD RULE referenced explicitly in the migration comment. Test Tests-H gates the e2e suite. Backups taken before migration runs in production.

risks.risk: Permission cache leak — a stale permission set lets a user keep revoked authority past a tenant's expectation. For external auditor roles, this is a compliance breach.
risks.mitigation: TTL capped at 60s. Role / role_permissions writes broadcast invalidation events to in-process listeners that drop affected cache entries immediately. Test Tests-F gates the e2e suite. Where stronger guarantees are needed (e.g. revocation during an active session), document a follow-up Phase X feature for NOTIFY/LISTEN realtime invalidation.

risks.risk: Cross-tenant role leak — a bug in the SELECT returning roles to a tenant exposes another tenant's custom roles. Catastrophic for trust.
risks.mitigation: Every roles query goes through a single repository function with the tenant filter baked in; review-required PR; penetration test (Tests-A) gates the e2e suite; lint rule flags any direct SELECT FROM roles outside the repository.

risks.risk: 21-file frontend refactor blast radius — a missed equality check returns true for the wrong role and exposes admin UI to a non-admin.
risks.mitigation: ESLint rule banning role === string-literal compares (CI gate); reviewer manually spot-checks the 21 files post-refactor; visual smoke test of all four system role logins (gadmin, padmin, team_lead, user) gates the deploy.

risks.risk: RequireRole → RequirePermission refactor on 14+ routes mis-maps an intent — a route ends up requiring the wrong permission and either over-restricts (functional regression) or under-restricts (security regression).
risks.mitigation: Each refactor lands as a small commit referencing the catalogue code chosen; PR includes a before/after table; integration test suite must continue to pass for every prior gadmin/padmin role test; new tests added per route asserting the expected permission gate.

risks.risk: Permission catalogue drift — a refactor renames a Go permission constant without updating the seeded migration, or vice versa. Either side missing breaks the parity check at startup, blocking deploys.
risks.mitigation: Startup parity check is the gate — the server refuses to start if catalogue and DB diverge. Documentation explicitly requires both sides to be updated together. Adding a permission becomes a small migration + a small Go constant — both reviewed in the same PR.

risks.risk: Tenant rank collision — a tenant creates a custom role at rank 25 (matching P_admin), making role-ceiling produce confusing answers between the two roles.
risks.mitigation: CHECK constraint on roles.rank refuses tenant rows in the system bands [10, 20, 25, 30] (and band 5 reserved for external system seed). Tenant-custom rank must be in [11..24] or [26..29]. Validator on Create/Update returns a clear 422 error if violated. Documented in /admin/roles UI copy.

risks.risk: MVP scope creep — once gadmins see /admin/roles, requests for multi-role-per-user, named permission schemes, time-bound external-account expiry, and tri-state permissions arrive mid-implementation and threaten the cut.
risks.mitigation: Phase X deferrals are written into the plan (scope), the leaf doc, and acceptance criterion #5. Story titles for MVP work include the suffix '(MVP)' so triage sees the cut. Phase X work gets its own future plan; requests get parked there, not folded into PLA-0007.

risks.risk: Migration Z (dropping users.role) is forgotten or runs prematurely — leaving the enum in place forever or breaking rollback before the observation window closes.
risks.mitigation: Migration Z is documented as deferred; a calendar reminder for one full release cycle is added; rollback playbook covers reverting role_id reads back to users.role within the window. Z runs as its own PR after monitoring confirms zero reads of users.role across the application.

risks.risk: Performance of cold-cache permission lookup at high QPS — three-table join adds milliseconds to first-request latency on a fresh session.
risks.mitigation: Warm cache on session create; index on role_permissions(role_id) for the hot lookup; benchmark before launch with realistic tenant data (50 custom roles × 30 permissions × 1,000 fresh sessions/min). If cold-cache cost exceeds budget, materialise a denormalised role_permission_codes(role_id, codes text[]) materialised view refreshed on role_permissions write.

references.label: R030 — Custom Roles & Permissions Architecture: Blockers and Prior Art (this plan's evidence base)
PLA-0005 — Universal addressable element registry (substrate /admin/roles adopts)
feedback_role_ceiling.md — role-ceiling rule referenced in models.go
Microsoft Learn — About Permissions, Access Levels, and Security Groups (Azure DevOps)
PostgreSQL Documentation — ALTER TYPE (enum value addition limitations)
